concise_definition: Baseline out of bounds plays : Sets that feature one offensive player that will inbound the basketball and four additional offensive players that will execute actions such as cuts or screens to generate potential scoring opportunities at the rim or from the perimeter.

sections.heading: What is a baseline out of bounds play in basketball?
sections.text: A baseline out of bounds play features one player who will inbound the basketball by standing near the baseline on the opposite side of the basket and four additional players who will execute different offensive actions to generate potential scoring opportunities at the rim or from the perimeter.

sections.heading: What are examples of box baseline out of bounds plays?
sections.text: Box baseline out of bounds plays could feature several screening actions, initiated from thebox offense.

sections.heading: Box baseline out of bounds play – Example 1
sections.text:
Players could set several screens to help other teammates get open, resulting in potential scoring possibilities near the basket or near the corner perimeter area.

The example diagram below demonstrates this concept.

[Image Alt Text: An example of a baseline out of bounds play initiated from the box formation.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of a baseline out of bounds play initiated from the box formation.]

To begin, 1 cuts to the left side corner via thescreensset by 3 and 4.

Next, 3 cuts to the basket via theback screenset by 5. Following that, 1 could receive the ball and take the open jump shot or 3 could receive the ball and score near the basket.

sections.heading: Box baseline out of bounds play – Example 2
sections.text:
Two players could set screens across the lane and high post area during the initial inbound pass phase. Afterwards, another screen could be set for the inbound player, leading to an additional scoring option.

The example diagram below illustrates this in more detail.

[Image Alt Text: Box Baseline Out - Example 2 - Part 1]

[Image Alt Text: Box Baseline Out - Example 2 - Part 1]

To start, 1 cuts to the left side corner via the cross screen set by 5. At the same time, 3 cuts to the left side wing via the high post screen set by 4.

Next, 1 receives the ball from 2 and can take the jump shot if open. If 1 is not open, then 1 could swing the ball to 3.

Also, after setting the screens, 4 cuts to the top while 5 cuts to the right side low post area.

[Image Alt Text: Box Baseline Out - Example 2 - Part 2]

[Image Alt Text: Box Baseline Out - Example 2 - Part 2]

Next, 4 receives the ball from 3 and as that occurs, 2 cuts to the right side wing via the pin down screen set by 5. After that, 2 receives the ball from 4 and can take the jump shot if open.

If 2 is not open, then 5 could cut back to the right side low post after setting the screen and receive the ball from 2. Following that, 5 could score near the basket with a low post move.

sections.heading: Box baseline out of bounds play – Example 3
sections.text:
A player could use an elevator screen to get open and receive the ball. Following that, the inbound player could also get open by way of a stagger screen.

The example diagrams below demonstrate this with more clarity.

[Image Alt Text: Box Baseline Out - Example 3 - Part 1]

[Image Alt Text: Box Baseline Out - Example 3 - Part 1]

To begin, 1 cuts to the left side corner viaelevator screenset by 3 and 5.

Afterwards, 1 could receive the ball from 2 and take the open three-point jump shot. If 1 is not open, then 3 can cut to the top while 5 cuts to the right side mid-post area.

[Image Alt Text: Box Baseline Out - Example 3 - Part 2]

[Image Alt Text: Box Baseline Out - Example 3 - Part 2]

Next, 1 dribbles toward the free throw line area via the corner ball screen set by 3. At the same time, 2 cuts to the right side wing via thestagger screenset by 4 and 5.

Following that, 1 could take the open mid-range jump shot or 2 could receive the ball from 1 and then take the open three-point jump shot.

sections.heading: What are examples of stack baseline out of bounds plays?
sections.text: Stack baseline out of bounds plays primarily feature various screens to help players get open, initiated from a stack set.

sections.heading: Stack baseline out of bounds play – Example 1
sections.text:
Two players could set screens while two other players use those screens to get open near the basket and near a perimeter area, respectively.

The example diagram below demonstrates this with more details.

[Image Alt Text: An example of a baseline out of bounds play initiated from the stack formation.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of a baseline out of bounds play initiated from the stack formation.]

To start, 5 cuts to the basket via the back screen set by 3. Also, at the same time, 1 cuts to the right side corner via the back screen set by 4.

Following that, 5 could receive the ball from 2 and score near the basket or 1 could receive the ball from 2 and take the jump shot if open.

sections.heading: Stack baseline out of bounds play – Example 2
sections.text:
A player could cut to the ball side corner via a stagger screen while one of the screeners gets open via yet another screen, leading to potential scoring options. This is alsoscreen the screeneraction.

The example diagram below demonstrates this concept.

[Image Alt Text: Stack Baseline Out - Example 2]

[Image Alt Text: Stack Baseline Out - Example 2]

To start, 3 cuts to the left side corner via the stagger screen set by 4 and 5.

Following that, 5 cuts to the basket via the back screen set by 2. Next, 3 could receive the ball from 1 and take the open jump shot or 5 could receive the ball from 1 and score at the rim.

sections.heading: What are examples of baseline out of bounds zone offense plays?
sections.text: Baseline out of bounds zone offense plays generally consists of players trying to exploit zone gaps, particularly by cutting into those vulnerable areas and/or by setting screens for other teammates.

sections.heading: Baseline out of bounds zone offense play – Example 1
sections.text:
Two players that are initially near the high post elbows can cut in an X-pattern towards the low post areas while two other wing players could cut to the adjacent corners. This could lead to multiple scoring options as the zone defenders try to decide who to guard.

The example diagram below illustrates this in more detail. Also, as an aside, the defense is set up in a2-3 zone.

[Image Alt Text: An example of a baseline out of bounds play vs. 2-3 zone defense.]

[Image Alt Text: An example of a baseline out of bounds play vs. 2-3 zone defense.]

To begin, 5 cuts across to the right side low post. At the same time, 4 cuts across to the left side low post.

Following that, 2 cuts down to the left side corner while at the same time, 3 cuts down to the right side corner.

When this occurs, the three backside defenders in particular, who are X3, X4, and X5, would now have the task of trying to guard four offensive players.

Therefore, in theory, someone should be open and from that point, 1 would pass the ball to any of the other players depending on who would be open.

sections.heading: Baseline out of bounds zone offense play – Example 2
sections.text:
A player who is initially near the high post area on ball side could cut through to the weak side corner via screens set by other teammates.

Also, a wing player on ball side could cut to the adjacent corner while one of the screeners slips into a gap near the ball side low post area.

The zone defenders have to determine what to possibly give up; the high percentage shot near the basket or an open three-point shot on either side.

The example diagram below showcases this with more clarity. It should be noted as well that the defense implements a 2-3 zone, similar to the previous diagram.

[Image Alt Text: Zone Baseline Out - Example 2]

[Image Alt Text: Zone Baseline Out - Example 2]

To start, 2 cuts to the right side corner via the screens set by 5 and 4. Next, 1 cuts to the left side corner and 5 cuts to the left side low post area after setting the screen.

Following that, 1 or 2 could receive the ball and take three-point shots if open or 5 could receive the ball instead and score near the basket.
